components.name: Button
components.description: Triggers primary, secondary, text, icon-only, block, and loading actions.
anti_patterns: Do not use blue as default primary action color.
Do not place multiple competing primary buttons in one action group.

components.name: Input
components.description: Text entry component for forms and search-like input scenarios.
usage_rules: Use #222222 for hover and focus border.
anti_patterns: Do not separate validation feedback from the input.
Do not use borderless input where the surrounding structure is unclear.

components.name: Search
components.description: Search input with optional clear, batch, autocomplete, custom popup, and history behavior.
usage_rules: Follow Input border and size rules.
Use upper-layer shadow for autocomplete and custom popups.
Keep popup visually attached to the input.
anti_patterns: Do not display detached popup content without a clear relationship to the search input.

components.name: Form
components.description: Structured data entry pattern composed from inputs, selects, radios, checkboxes, switches, textareas, tooltips, and buttons.
anti_patterns: Do not use inconsistent label widths within one form section.
Do not place validation messages far from their controls.

components.name: Alert
components.description: Inline notice block for information that needs user attention.
usage_rules: Use Alert for inline persistent notices.
anti_patterns: Do not use Alert as a floating operation notification; use Message instead.

components.name: Message
components.description: Lightweight global feedback after user operations.
anti_patterns: Do not use Message for persistent inline content or complex actions.

components.name: Dialog
components.description: Temporary focused window for user response without replacing the full task flow.
anti_patterns: Do not use Dialog for contextual side workflows; use Drawer.

components.name: Table
components.description: Structured data display for organization, comparison, search, filtering, sorting, and analysis.
usage_rules: Use 40px as default header and row height.
Use Tooltip for truncated cell content.
Show active filter state and clear action.
anti_patterns: Do not use arbitrary row heights outside observed density options.
Do not hide active filters without visible condition display.

components.name: Tag
components.description: Lightweight marker for classification, selection, or business status.
usage_rules: Use semantic tag colors for success/warning/danger.
Use 4px icon/text gap and 8px text/close gap.
anti_patterns: Do not use tags as primary action buttons.
Do not invent new semantic colors when existing tag themes cover the status.

components.name: SidebarNavigation
components.description: Persistent left-side navigation for dashboard and workbench pages.
variants: top-level with icon and collapse arrow

sizes.sidebar_width_rule: adaptive; 160 is historical narrow reference only, not the default for generated workbench pages
sizes.nav_content_width_rule: fill available sidebar width minus inner padding; preserve rhythm and alignment rather than fixed width
sizes.top_level_label_rule: fill remaining width; do not clip labels; widen, collapse, or truncate with tooltip
sizes.selected_sub_item_width_rule: fill available nav content width; preserve white surface and red marker

usage_rules: Use #FFFFFF as the navigation surface when the right content area also uses light cards.
Use sidebar_elevated shadow or a clear divider so the left navigation and right content hierarchy are separated.
Use #F5F7F9 as app background behind elevated surfaces.
Use Auto Layout for the navigation column and each menu item.
Use adaptive width, collapsed mode, or truncation with tooltip for long labels.
On generated desktop workbench pages, prefer 200-256px sidebar width; on high-resolution pages, use 240px or more when it improves hierarchy and spacing.
anti_patterns: Do not leave the sidebar as flat gray-on-gray when the content area is also gray; add white surface, shadow, divider, or spacing.
Do not use 160px as the default generated sidebar width for broad desktop pages.
Do not force every future sidebar to 160px.
Do not clip Chinese labels to preserve observed reference widths.
Do not squeeze the content gap below 24px on wide pages.
Do not use #222222 as selected background in this sidebar style.
Do not replace the 4px red selected dot with a full-height bar unless a future Figma source confirms that variant.
Do not use oversized icons or 14px labels for this navigation style.

components.name: TopNavigation
components.description: Top application header for page title, global actions, user identity, and optional horizontal menu.
usage_rules: Use TopNavigation when a page has global actions such as create task, user profile, or horizontal menu.
Keep top navigation on a white or clearly separated surface.
Separate it from content with subtle border or topbar_subtle shadow.
Keep page title on the left and global actions/user area on the right for workbench pages.
Use #222222 for primary action buttons in the topbar.
anti_patterns: Do not let content start at the very top without a header when the page has global actions.
Do not mix top navigation and page section tabs without clear hierarchy.
Do not make the topbar visually heavier than the main page content.

components.name: Drawer
components.description: Contextual side workflow container observed in task recommendation and communication follow-up flows.
usage_rules: Use for contextual side workflows that should keep the user in the current page.
Use forms, descriptions, alerts, and action footer as needed.
anti_patterns: Do not use Dialog for long side workflows that match Drawer behavior.
